summary: Ukrainian forces report intensified UAV and Shahed attacks across multiple fronts, targeting Russian-held infrastructure and airfields, while Russian strikes continue against Ukrainian energy sites and rear areas. New reactive UAV groups are entering northern Chernihiv and Zhytomyr regions, increasing pressure on border zones. Both sides claim significant losses of aircraft and drones, indicating a high‑intensity aerial contest.
key_points: Ukrainian reactive UAVs infiltrate north Chernihiv via Klimovo.
Shahed drones massed on Dnipro corridor, with up to 26 reported.
Russian strikes hit Ukrainian energy facilities in Enerhodar and Zaporizhzhia.
Ukrainian forces down 108 Russian drones in a single day.
New UAV groups operating from the Black Sea toward Odesa region.
Russian air assets Ka-27 and Be-200 destroyed at Yeysk airfield.
Heavy civilian stress and medical attacks reported, affecting morale.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian reactive UAVs have breached the northern Chernihiv border, expanding the UAV threat corridor toward Kyiv. Simultaneously, Russian forces continue high‑tempo strikes on Ukrainian energy infrastructure and rear areas, while both sides report heavy drone losses. The aerial battlefield remains highly contested, with Shahed swarms and reactive UAVs intensifying pressure on multiple fronts.

points: Reactive UAVs entered north Chernihiv via Klimovo, indicating a new penetration route for Ukrainian strike assets (Source: @eRadarrua/92445).
Ukrainian Shahed swarms of up to 26 drones were observed over northern Vinnytsia heading toward Khmelnytskyi, with vectors also targeting Starokostiantyniv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66815).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying 108 Russian drones in a 24‑hour period, reflecting robust air‑defence performance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian attacks struck the Russian military airfield Belbek in Crimea and damaged air‑defence assets there (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks hit Russian energy sites in Enerhodar and the Zaporizhzhia plant area, aiming to degrade power generation (Source: @tass_agency/376366).
Russian forces destroyed a Ukrainian Ka‑27 helicopter and a Be‑200 maritime patrol aircraft at Yeysk airfield in Krasnodar Krai (Source: @UkraineNow/81293).

Russian strikes targeted Ukrainian infrastructure in Odesa, Krivoy Rog, Dnipropetrovsk, Starokostiantyniv, Rivne, Kirovohrad and Kremenchuk, employing both UAVs and missile systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian Shahed attacks on the Belgorod direction caused civilian casualties, including a head injury in Otradnoe and a fatal truck strike in Nezhegol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian Ministry of Defense reported extensive UAV activity in rear regions, indicating a broadening of the aerial threat envelope (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian forces claimed capture of the village of Borovaya in Kharkiv region, aiming to close a pocket near the Oskol River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian Shahed drones from Kharkiv moved toward Bogoduhiv, increasing pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62145).

Russian reports of Shahed attacks from Sumy direction targeting Shostka district, with Ukrainian assault groups claiming to destroy combat groups and capture four soldiers (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAV groups from eastern Kharkiv headed toward Chuhuiv, indicating continued reconnaissance and strike activity in the Donbas sector (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Russian forces reported advancing up to 150 m near the village of Granovo on the Kharkiv front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks from Zaporizhzhia on Dnipropetrovsk district continued, with multiple drones reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
New reactive UAV groups entered north Chernihiv, moving along the Belarus border toward Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/62170).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Zhytomyr heading toward Sarny (Rivne) and Korosten, expanding the northern strike envelope (Source: @eRadarrua/92432).
Shahed swarms from Sumy region targeted Shostka and surrounding villages, with Ukrainian forces claiming captures (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported near Gorbuny and Gora (Source: @eRadarrua/92443).
Russian Shahed attacks from Sumy on the Priluksky district of Chernihiv caused a circulating drone over the area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Ukrainian Shahed drones from Kherson moved toward Kryvyi Rih, indicating pressure on the southern industrial belt (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Russian strikes hit Odesa and Krivoy Rog, targeting logistics hubs in the south (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea headed toward Odesa region, suggesting a new maritime‑to‑land strike vector (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Russian air attacks on the Rostov region resulted in UAV losses in Taganrog, Kamensk and Azov districts, reflecting broader southern air activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian forces reported attacks on Russian-held oil facilities in Moscow region, including the Angstrom semiconductor plant (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
Ukrainian forces shot down 108 Russian drones in a single day, showing effective air‑defence (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian Shahed attacks on Ukrainian territory were reported across multiple fronts, with clusters of 20‑26 drones targeting Dnipro and Vinnytsia (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66815, @mon1tor_ua/66795).
Ukrainian reactive UAVs are now operating from the north, increasing the density of low‑altitude threats (Source: @eRadarrua/92445).
Russian air assets Ka‑27 and Be‑200 were destroyed at Yeysk, reducing Russian maritime air capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81293).
Russian missile and UAV strikes on Ukrainian rear areas continued, with reported attacks on logistics nodes in Dnipro and Kirovohrad (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).

key_developments: Ukrainian reactive UAVs breached the northern Chernihiv border via Klimovo, opening a new strike corridor toward Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/92445).
Russian forces destroyed a Ukrainian Ka‑27 helicopter and a Be‑200 patrol aircraft at Yeysk airfield, degrading Ukrainian air capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81293).
Ukrainian air‑defences reported downing 108 Russian drones in a 24‑hour period, indicating heightened defensive effectiveness (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Mass Shahed drone formations of up to 26 were observed over northern Vinnytsia heading toward Khmelnytskyi, stressing regional air‑defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66815).
Russian strikes hit Ukrainian energy infrastructure in Enerhodar and Zaporizhzhia, aiming to disrupt power supply (Source: @tass_agency/376366).
New UAV groups from the Black Sea moved toward Odesa, expanding maritime‑to‑land attack options for Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Ukrainian forces reported attacks on Russian military airfield Belbek in Crimea and on Russian defence assets in Moscow region (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian reactive UAVs and Shahed swarms are increasing pressure on Russian rear areas, especially along the northern border and southern Odesa corridor, while Russian air strikes continue to target Ukrainian energy and logistics hubs. The high volume of drone engagements suggests both sides are heavily reliant on UAVs for offensive and defensive operations, raising the risk of rapid attrition. Continued Russian attempts to degrade Ukrainian air assets, exemplified by the Yeysk losses, indicate a focus on air superiority, but Ukrainian robust air‑defence performance mitigates this threat for now.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are monitoring the surge in UAV activity, with increased intelligence sharing on drone trajectories and counter‑UAV technologies. Russia is seeking additional foreign support for its air‑defence and drone production, as indicated by diplomatic engagements in China and India. Ukraine is likely to request further air‑defence systems to counter the growing Shahed threat, while also leveraging new reactive UAV capabilities to pressure Russian logistics.
sections.intelligence_notes: The emergence of reactive UAV groups in northern Chernihiv suggests a shift in Ukrainian operational focus toward border‑area disruption. Russian Shahed deployments remain concentrated on strategic infrastructure, requiring sustained air‑defence allocation. The reported civilian stress and attacks on medical personnel could impact morale and recruitment, underscoring the need for humanitarian and psychological operations support.
